xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: B-8 MIDC Industrial Area Waluj- 431136,District- Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar (Aurangabad), Maharashtra
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: FDC House, C-11 &12, Dalia Industrial Estate, Oshiwara Village, Off Link Road, Near Fun Republic, Andheri (West), Mumbai-400053
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfContactPerson: Ms. Varsharani Katre (Company Secretary & Legal Head)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: Manufacturing and marketing of pharmaceutical products
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProductOrServiceSoldByTheEntit: Manufacture of pharmaceuticals, medicinal and chemical products
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: Operating in the pharmaceutical sector, we primarily serve a diverse range of external stakeholders, including wholesalers, suppliers, customers, employees, and government institutions. Our key customer groups and partners include: • Wholesalers and distributors who handle bulk supply of pharmaceutical products to pharmacies, hospitals, and clinics • Suppliers of active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), packaging materials, and laboratory equipment essential for drug manufacturing • Healthcare institutions such as hospitals, clinics, and pharmacies that purchase medicines and healthcare products for patient use • Government bodies and regulatory agencies involved in the approval, monitoring, and procurement of pharmaceutical products
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheHoldingOrSubsidiaryAs: Fair Deal Corporation Pharmaceutical SA (Pty) Limited, South Africa
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: Investigations completed and awaiting for closure
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Regulatory pressures on carbon emissions Extreme weather disruptions Investors focus on decarbonization
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Transition to renewable energy sources (solar, biomass) • Energy-efficient manufacturing systems • Process optimization for emissions reduction. • Monitoring and disclosure of GHG emissions
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risks: • Resource scarcity (water, energy) • Regulatory penalties for emissions, effluent discharge, improper waste disposal • Operational disruptions due to energy/water constraints Opportunities: • Cost reduction through efficiency improvements • Circular economy practices open new revenue streams. • Positive brand perception and stakeholder confidence from sustainable operations Risks: • Resource scarcity (water, energy) • Regulatory penalties for emissions, effluent discharge, improper waste disposal • Operational disruptions due to energy/water constraints Opportunities: • Cost reduction through efficiency improvements • Circular economy practices open new revenue streams. • Positive brand perception and stakeholder confidence from sustainable operations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Mitigate Risks: • Invest in Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) systems. • Upgrade effluent treatment and recycling infrastructure. • Optimize energy consumption via energy-efficient equipment. • Implement strict hazardous waste disposal protocols. Leverage Opportunities: • Expand renewable energy (solar rooftops, biomass boilers) • Conduct energy and water audits. • Adopt 4R principles (Reduce, Reuse, Recycle, Recharge) • Explore waste-to-energy and resource recovery. • Strengthen secondary raw material markets via recycling initiatives. • Communicate operational sustainability initiatives to customers/investors
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: • Demand for eco-friendly packaging • Regulatory requirements on product sustainability • Stakeholder expectations for responsible production
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: • Redesign packaging to reduce plastic use. • Adopt sustainable raw materials. • Obtain Environmental Product Declarations (EPD) • Promote green chemistry in product development
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: • Operations near sensitive ecosystems • Land-use impacts from expansions. • Increasing regulatory oversight on biodiversity
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: • Conduct biodiversity impact assessments. • Develop greenbelt zones around facilities. • Regular afforestation and habitat restoration • Monitor biodiversity impacts periodically
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: • Industrial accidents • Natural disasters impacting operations. • Safety and business continuity concerns
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: • Develop and update emergency response plans. • Conduct regular mock drills. • Employee safety and emergency training • Invest in early warning and mitigation systems
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: • Diverse teams drive innovation and productivity. • Inclusive work culture strengthens employer brand. • Regulatory and market expectations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: • Inclusive hiring practices • Focus on women and minority representation. • Conduct DEI awareness and sensitization programs. • Implement fair career progression policies
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: • Workplace accidents • Occupational health risks • Legal liabilities from poor safety standards
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: • Implement OHS management systems. • Conduct safety audits and trainings. • Monitor and report safety KPIs. • Employee health and wellness initiatives
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: • Strengthens community relationships. • Supports long-term license to operate. • Regulatory CSR spend requirements
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: • Focus CSR on healthcare access, education, livelihood programs. • Engage local communities through partnerships. • Track CSR impact and align to SDGs
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: • Societal demand for affordable medicines • Expanding into underserved marketsGovernment focus on public health outcomes
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: • Focus on cost-effective formulations. • Expand reach in rural and semi-urban markets. • Collaborate with public health agencies for distribution. Ensure pricing transparency and affordability focus
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: • Non-compliance risks • Regulatory penalties • Investor distrust
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: • Strengthen board oversight. • Conduct ethics and compliance training. • Regular internal audits and reviews • Whistleblower mechanisms
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: • Strong risk management ensures resilience. • Supports operational continuity. • Enhances stakeholder trust
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: • Strengthen enterprise risk management (ERM) systems. • Periodic risk assessments across functions • Build scenario planning and contingency strategies. • Integrate ESG risks into ERM
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Opportunities: • Digitalization drives operational efficiency and cost optimization. • Enhances product innovation and customer engagement. • Supports market competitiveness through agile operations. Risks: • Data breaches pose financial and reputational risks. • Non-compliance with data protection regulations can result in penalties
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM:
Leverage Opportunities: • Invest in smart manufacturing and automation. • Adopt digital platforms for supply chain and operations management. • Implement digital engagement and e-commerce platforms. • Use data analytics for strategic decision-making
Mitigate Risks: • Strengthen cybersecurity infrastructure. • Conduct regular vulnerability assessments. • Roll out cyber awareness training for all employees. • Develop and test incident response and data recovery plans
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheNationalAndInternatio: FDC Limited has received the following certifications: Waluj Plant: Major approvals are US-FDA, MHRA and PIC/s (Malaysia). We also have approvals from other countries as well (MCAZ, Tanzania, Kenya, Ethiopia, Uganda, Sudan etc.).Waluj has also been approved by Ukraine in 2023 Goa I Plant and Goa 3 plant: Approved by MHRA and PIC/s Malaysia, MCAZ Sinnar Plant: Local WHO, UNICEF / MSF, Ethiopia Baddi Plant: PIC/S Malaysia, US-FDA Roha Plant: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health & Safety Management System and ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management System Certification by Indian Register Quality Systems And complying the quality by GMP practice

FDC Limited has received the following certifications: Waluj Plant: Major approvals are US-FDA, MHRA and PIC/s (Malaysia). We also have approvals from other countries as well (MCAZ, Tanzania, Kenya, Ethiopia, Uganda, Sudan etc.).Waluj has also been approved by Ukraine in 2023 Goa I Plant and Goa 3 plant: Approved by MHRA and PIC/s Malaysia, MCAZ Sinnar Plant: Local WHO, UNICEF / MSF, Ethiopia Baddi Plant: PIC/S Malaysia, US-FDA Roha Plant: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health & Safety Management System and ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management System Certification by Indian Register Quality Systems And complying the quality by GMP practice

We also have approvals from other countries as well (MCAZ, Tanzania, Kenya, Ethiopia, Uganda, Sudan etc.).Waluj has also been approved by Ukraine in 2023 Goa I Plant and Goa 3 plant: Approved by MHRA and PIC/s Malaysia, MCAZ Sinnar Plant: Local WHO, UNICEF / MSF, Ethiopia Baddi Plant: PIC/S Malaysia, US-FDA Roha Plant: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health & Safety Management System and ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management System Certification by Indian Register Quality Systems And complying the quality by GMP practice

We also have approvals from other countries as well (MCAZ, Tanzania, Kenya, Ethiopia, Uganda, Sudan etc.).Waluj has also been approved by Ukraine in 2023 Goa I Plant and Goa 3 plant: Approved by MHRA and PIC/s Malaysia, MCAZ Sinnar Plant: Local WHO, UNICEF / MSF, Ethiopia Baddi Plant: PIC/S Malaysia, US-FDA Roha Plant: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health & Safety Management System and ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management System Certification by Indian Register Quality Systems And complying the quality by GMP practice

We also have approvals from other countries as well (MCAZ, Tanzania, Kenya, Ethiopia, Uganda, Sudan etc.).Waluj has also been approved by Ukraine in 2023 Goa I Plant and Goa 3 plant: Approved by MHRA and PIC/s Malaysia, MCAZ Sinnar Plant: Local WHO, UNICEF / MSF, Ethiopia Baddi Plant: PIC/S Malaysia, US-FDA Roha Plant: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health & Safety Management System and ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management System Certification by Indian Register Quality Systems And complying the quality by GMP practice

We also have approvals from other countries as well (MCAZ, Tanzania, Kenya, Ethiopia, Uganda, Sudan etc.).Waluj has also been approved by Ukraine in 2023 Goa I Plant and Goa 3 plant: Approved by MHRA and PIC/s Malaysia, MCAZ Sinnar Plant: Local WHO, UNICEF / MSF, Ethiopia Baddi Plant: PIC/S Malaysia, US-FDA Roha Plant: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health & Safety Management System and ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management System Certification by Indian Register Quality Systems And complying the quality by GMP practice

We also have approvals from other countries as well (MCAZ, Tanzania, Kenya, Ethiopia, Uganda, Sudan etc.).Waluj has also been approved by Ukraine in 2023 Goa I Plant and Goa 3 plant: Approved by MHRA and PIC/s Malaysia, MCAZ Sinnar Plant: Local WHO, UNICEF / MSF, Ethiopia Baddi Plant: PIC/S Malaysia, US-FDA Roha Plant: ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health & Safety Management System and ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management System Certification by Indian Register Quality Systems And complying the quality by GMP practice
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar: We are in the process of setting goals and targets related to Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) factors, supported by a comprehensive ESG roadmap. This plan will outline key initiatives and timelines to ensure accountability and track our progress in achieving sustainability and governance objectives

We are in the process of setting goals and targets related to Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) factors, supported by a comprehensive ESG roadmap. This plan will outline key initiatives and timelines to ensure accountability and track our progress in achieving sustainability and governance objectives

We are in the process of setting goals and targets and performance will be evaluated in the upcoming reporting years.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: Refer “Our ESG Journey unfolded” which is part of the Annual Report
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe: Name : Mr. Mohan A. Chandavarkar Designation : Managing Director Telephone Number : 022-26739100 Email Id : investors@fdcindia.com
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: The Company has constituted an ESG Committee comprising all the important functional heads for ESG initiatives. The Committee is responsible for aligning the Company’s ESG vision, mission, and goals with Corporate Strategy. The Committee shall advise Management on ESG strategies, recommend policy amendments.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: Life & Breath / Aquasail / Generative AI / Prevention of sexual Harassment / Fire Fighting / Cyber Security / Pharmacovigilance /

Life & Breath / Leadership & Soft Skill / Generative AI/ ASPIRE Training / Occupational health & safety / Artificial Intelligence / Prevention of sexual Harassment / Fire Fighting / Cyber Security / Pharmacovigilance / Quality Management System / Iceberg / Good manufacturing practice / Data Integrity / CRISP / Hi - Fly / Step-up / Boost-up / I-Coach / I-Rise / I-Lead / Synergy / QSP/Personal Hygiene
Occupational health & safety / Prevention of sexual Harassment / Fire Fighting / Good manufacturing practice/Personal Hygiene
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheRegulatoryOrEnforceme: Deputy Commissioner of State Tax, Kalbadevi, Mumbai
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:BriefOfTheMonetaryCaseForPenal: Mismatch of ITC (GSTR-3B Vs GSTR-2A) and short payment of tax due to misclassification of HSN Code
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheCase: In relation to the instance mentioned in the question above, an appeal has been preferred against the demand order issued under Section 73 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, as communicated through Form DRC-07. This appeal addresses the concerns regarding the mismatch of ITC and related tax payment issues and is currently under review by the appropriate authorities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheRegulatoryOrEnforceme: GST COUNCIL- GOODS AND SERVICES TAX
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol:
FDC Limited has an Anti-Corruption and Anti-Bribery Policy embedded within its Code of Conduct. The policy is aligned with applicable laws and regulations and applies to all directors, senior management, and employees. It strictly prohibits any involvement in corrupt practices, including offering, giving, receiving, or soliciting bribes in the form of money, services, valuables, or other benefits to influence administrative or official processes. These guidelines are applicable to dealings with individuals and organizations, including government bodies, local authorities, officials, and private entities, whether directly or through intermediaries.
The full policy can be accessed at: https://www.fdcindia.com/pdf/policies/Code_of_ Conduct_of_FDC_Limited.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse:
Yes, FDC Limited’s Code of Conduct outlines that directors are expected to avoid any activity that may give rise to a conflict with the Company’s best interests. As a part of good governance practices, directors are required to annually confirm their compliance with the Code.
Policy: https://www.fdcindia.com/corporate-governance#Policies
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImprovementsInEnviron: Roha: Installed scrubber system in bromine storage to control harmful gas emissions; set up sewage treatment plant for wastewater treatment and water conservation. Sinnar: Replaced electric heating with briquette-fired boilers for AHUs & Nauta mixers, reducing electricity use and carbon emissions; installed effluent storage tank for safe wastewater handling. Goa-3: Installed 1.5-ton briquette-fired steam boiler with cyclone separator and air pre-heater for clean combustion, energy efficiency, and reduced air pollution. New Ghaziabad and Ghaziabad Warehouses: Installed 100 KVA diesel generators compliant with CPCB-IV+ norms, reducing particulate and NOx emissions in line with Delhi NCR environmental standards.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: Plastic waste from each plant is collected by authorized scrap dealers registered with the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) for plastic waste. Materials that are multilayer (containing some plastic) are shredded before handing over to the scrap dealer. The details of the amount of plastic used and given for recycling by consumers are managed by RE Sustainability, a company partnered with FDC for EPR management. Additionally, plastic components used carry the plastic Resin Identification Code on the bottom of the container (e.g., 1 for PET, 2 for HDPE) to ensure proper recycling.
Sold to authorised vendor for safe disposal

In the event of expired material or damaged products resulting from improper handling, we adhere to a strict and comprehensive recall process. This process includes retrieval of the affected products and appropriate handling and destruction of the returned items to ensure safety and compliance
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic:
an equal opportunity policy that reflects our commitment to diversity and inclusion. We aim to create a supportive work environment where every individual is valued and respected, ensuring that all employees can thrive without facing any form of discrimination.
Policy: https://www.fdcindia.com/corporate-governance#Policies
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR: Grievances are identified, discussed, and communicated with the HR team
Grievances are identified, discussed, and communicated with the HR team
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: To ensure the environmental health and safety of our employees and the surrounding areas, we have implemented a comprehensive Environmental and Occupational Safety and Health Management System (OSHMS). Our system includes fully equipped Occupational Health Centres (OHCs), fire pump houses, fire detection systems, and environmental treatment plants at all our locations. We also maintain an ambulance or emergency vehicle on-site for prompt medical response. A Factory Medical Officer (FMO) visits our plants regularly, and a full-time paramedical nurse is stationed at each location. This dedicated medical team is committed to providing the highest level of care to our workforce. Additionally, we conduct regular training sessions led by experts for fire fighters and first aiders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: We carry out regular safety audits through external third-party experts as well as internal inspections to ensure full compliance with our EHS system. These audits cover a wide range of areas, including Occupational Health and Safety (OHS), Electrical Safety, and Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA). Based on audit findings, we take corrective actions and implement all suggestions to maintain the highest safety standards. We also update our emergency evacuation plans as needed, reinforcing our ongoing commitment to a safe and secure work environment. Our Roha (API) site is certified under ISO 14001 for Environmental Management and ISO 45001 for Occupational Health and Safety.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: We have implemented comprehensive Environment Health and Safety policies that demonstrate our commitment to maintaining a safe work environment. Regular risk assessments, HIRA, HAZOP, etc. are carried out to identify and address potential workplace hazards. Employees receive extensive training on safety protocols, covering proper equipment use, emergency response procedures, and the safe handling of chemicals, Machine handling, work permit system etc. related to EHS. We actively encourage employees to promptly report any safety concerns or incidents. Regular reviews of safety practices and incident reports are conducted to continuously identify areas for improvement and enhance workplace safety.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: All observations fall under the minor category and have either been rectified or are currently in the process of being addressed. There are no significant risks identified, and none of the observations pose any immediate or material threat to operations or compliance. We continue to monitor and resolve all findings in a timely and systematic manner.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: To ensure compliance with statutory requirements, we have implemented a structured process to verify that all statutory dues are properly deducted and deposited by our value chain partners. We obtain necessary documentation from our partners as evidence of compliance. In cases where such documentation is not provided within the stipulated timelines, we take proactive measures to ensure full compliance and discharge of our responsibilities under relevant laws
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: No significant risks were identified during our assessments of health and safety practices and working conditions of value chain partners. Minor issues have been promptly addressed, and we continue to monitor compliance regularly.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif:
At FDC Limited, we identify our key stakeholder groups based on their influence on and interest in our business operations,as well as the value they contribute to our value chain. This assessment is carried out through internal discussions, crossfunctional inputs, and regular interactions across departments such as corporate affairs, human resources, procurement, sales,and regulatory affairs.
We prioritise stakeholders who are directly impacted by our operations or whose decisions significantly affect our business.These include our employees, shareholders, suppliers, customers, healthcare professionals, regulatory bodies, government agencies, drug regulators, communities, and NGOs. Our engagement with these groups is guided by the nature and intensity of their relationship with us, helping us ensure that their expectations, concerns, and feedback are effectively understood and addressed. This structured approach enables us to maintain meaningful and responsive stakeholder relationships that support long-term value creation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta: We prioritise a well-structured and ongoing consultation process between our stakeholders and the Board to ensure that key economic, environmental, and social issues are effectively identified and addressed. Through regular engagement sessions, we gather diverse perspectives and concerns, which are carefully documented and analysed. This feedback is then presented to the highest levels of governance, enabling the Board to make informed decisions and shape strategies that reflect stakeholder interests and promote sustainable business practices. Additionally, when consultation is delegated to specific committees or teams, their findings and insights are systematically reported back to the Board to maintain transparency and accountability throughout the process
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn: At FDC, we actively use stakeholder consultation to identify and manage environmental and social topics. Through these consultations, we prioritize material ESG issues and subsequently develop strategies and action plans to address them effectively.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement: We have actively engaged with vulnerable and marginalized stakeholder groups by addressing their needs and concerns through our dedicated Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives. We are committed to creating meaningful impact and supporting the well-being and development of these communities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Business updates Operational performance Legal compliance
Regulatory clarification Compliance with laws and amendments
Partner identification Sustainable procurement Quality assurance, Timely delivery and Compliance monitoring
Community development CSR project execution Healthcare and education promotion
Product information Usage guidance Query resolution
Business updates Health & safety Career development Skill enhancement
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: Yes, we have a grievance redressal mechanism in place for internal stakeholders, which is applicable to all employees and workers. This mechanism provides a structured process for reporting grievances related to human rights issues, ensuring that concerns are addressed promptly and effectively.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: We have implemented a Sexual Harassment Policy to address complaints related to discrimination and harassment. This policy applies to all our employees and is supported by the establishment of an Internal Complaints Committee for the Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) in our workplace. Any employee, who reasonably believes they are experiencing sexual harassment, either directly or indirectly, may file a complaint with any member of the POSH committee. Complaints can be submitted in writing or via email to complaint.committee@fdcindia.com within three months of the incident.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: Not applicable, since no significant risks were identified
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfABusinessProcessBeing: We have not received any human rights grievances or complaints in the reporting year.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf: We did not undertake any Human Rights due diligence during the reporting period
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfCoverageAndImplementa: Yes, all our manufacturing sites follow a Zero Liquid Discharge mechanism, treating wastewater through primary, secondary and tertiary stages. At Baddi, Roha and Waluj, treated water is sent to the Common Effluent Treatment Plant as per local regulations. As Roha facility being an API plant, in addition to the above-mentioned treatment, advanced tertiary treatment technology ROMEE is being used for maintaining stringent treated water quality
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc: Yes, we are committed to reducing our greenhouse gas emissions. During the reporting period, we have installed 2.5 MWp rooftop solar across facilities along with other initiatives. We avoided approximately 2,300 tCO2e through various energy saving initiatives
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: FDC India has established effective operational waste management practices, including the safe transfer of waste from its Effluent Treatment Plant (ETP) and manufacturing processes to authorized Common Hazardous Waste Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facilities (CHWTSDF). All manufacturing sites are equipped with fully operational ETPs and Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs), ensuring that discharge water consistently complies with State Pollution Control Board standards. Additionally, the company responsibly disposes of e-waste through certified vendors, who provide disposal certificates upon successful completion of the process.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableEnvir: NA – Not Applicable as there were no non-compliances during the financial year.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSignificantDirectAndI: Our Goa facilities come under the Goa Coastal Regulation Zone. We have conducted environmental impact assessment study,and no significant biodiversity impact was identified
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableABusi: FDC understands the critical importance of a Business Continuity Plan (BCP) to ensure the smooth operation of business, especially during challenging times. We prioritize business continuity from both the perspective of operational sustainability and employee welfare. Currently, we have established and implemented an on-site emergency preparedness plan across all operational facilities. Going forward, we are in the process of the developing a holistic business continuity and disaster management plan covering other aspects of the operations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple6ExplanatoryText: This Question is not applicable to tjhe company
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: PV Solar system with a total capacity of 2.5 MWp has been installed across the FDC Limited Group
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Generation and utilization of green energy resources, thereby reducing the Carbon emission
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Use of Green fuel for Boilers
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Agro- waste-based fuel are used for boilers as a primary fuel
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Utilization of Green energy resources, thereby reducing the Carbon emission
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Most of the roofs are sandwiched Rockwool insulated to achieve temperature gradient up to 8 – 10 degrees Celsius
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Maintaining ambient conditions below 30 degree Celsius without Air conditioning, thereby savings in electricity consumption
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: a. ETP/STP treated water is being used for toilet flushing. b. RO reject from process WTP is used for toilet flushing
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Reduction is source water requirement and efficient use of wastewater
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: For any new project LED lights are used and for old facilities existing CFL/FTL/MVL/SVL are replaced with LED lights
Most of the utility Equipment’s such as HVAC Chillers, Air compressors, boilers, fans, pumps, motors installed are energy efficient
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Reduction in electricity and fuel consumption due to efficient design and minimal losses
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Cyclone separator used to separate out solid particulate matter from boiler flue gases
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Maximize air filtration of boiler flue gases to reduce environment pollution
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Dissolved air flotation unit separates emulsified items like oil, greases, ghee etc
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Output archived as clear effluent for further ETP treatment
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Dust collector separates and collects the dust generated during manufacturing and filling process
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Reduced dusting in product manufacturing and filling area
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Wet scrubbers are used to scrub the fumes/dust generated from the process equipment
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Exhausting the clean air into atmosphere
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheArea: Baddi, Goa, Jogeshwari, Kandivali, Roha, Sinnar and Waluj
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheTradeAndIndustryChamb: Association of Small and Medium Chemical Manufactures
Pharmaceutical Export Promotion Council of India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: We engage with community members through both email and in-person meetings, providing effective and accessible channels of communication. These interactions help us promptly receive and address any grievances raised by the community.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:CSRProject: Medical and Health facility for rehabilitation of street youth indulged in drug consumption through Society Undertaking poor people’s onus for rehabilitation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: We have implemented a comprehensive Complaint Management System to ensure timely resolution of consumer complaints related to product quality, adverse effects, and other concerns. We provide transparent and accessible grievance redressal mechanisms to effectively address our clients’ feedback. For adverse event reporting, consumers can visit our website at https://www.fdcindia.com/adverse-event-reporting. Complaints can also be registered via our toll-free number, 1800 266 9347, or by emailing drug.safety@fdcindia.com.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ReasonsForVoluntaryRecall: 1. The reason for the recall is based on a product complaints wherein the patients were unable to get the solution out of the bottle as spike of the cap was lodged in the nozzle of the product bottle. 2. Increase in related substances of 1-AL (Levocetirizine Tablet IP 5 mg & 10 mg) 3. 00S result reported for dissolution test during stability study (Condition 25°C/60%RH). PEPCIA-10 (Rabeprazole Gastro-Resistant Tablets IP 10 mg)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ReasonsForForcedRecall: 1. The sample does not conform to IP with respect to Relative Substances test as per report issued by Central Drug Laboratory, Kolkata for Clip 500 (Tranexamic Acid Tablets IP 500 mg) 2. The sample does not conform to IP with respect to content of Rabeprazole sodium as per report issued by Food Safety and Drug Administration, Office of the District Magistrate,Lucknow for PEPCIA-10 (Rabeprazole Gastro-Resistant Tablets IP 10 mg).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WeblinkWhereInformationOnProdu: Information about our products and services is available on our official website and other digital platforms. Stakeholders can access comprehensive details through our website: www.fdcindia.com
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo: The information label on each product provides consumers with essential details, including safe usage instructions, ingredient sourcing, composition, mechanism of action, potential interactions, side effects, and guidance on proper storage conditions, along with other important information
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsInPlaceToInformConsu: In accordance with the guidelines of the National Pharmaceutical Pricing Authority (NPPA), we inform consumers of any risk of disruption or discontinuation of essential services by issuing a public notice regarding the discontinuation of any scheduled formulation.
